name: Elite Trader Funding
description_short: Elite Trader Funding is a futures-only prop firm offering 6 evaluation types, 100% profit split on first $12,500, and accounts up to $300K with same-day funding options.
description_long:
## The Six-Path Problem Elite Trader Funding Solves

Most futures prop firms give you one evaluation and one set of rules. Elite Trader Funding gives you six evaluation types, and the actual decision when you sign up is not whether to trade with them but which of those six paths matches how you actually trade. That is the central trade-off here: more flexibility than nearly any competitor in the futures prop firm category, at the cost of a menu that takes real effort to decode before you spend a dollar.

Elite Trader Funding is a futures-only firm operating out of Wilmington, Delaware, founded in 2022. It has grown quickly by leaning hard into promotional pricing and by adding evaluation formats that other firms did not offer. The company is not trying to be a general trading platform. It is trying to be the widest funnel in futures prop, and if you trade anything other than futures contracts, this review can end here.

## Picking Between the 1-Step, Fast Track, Diamond Hands and Direct to Funded

The base entry into Elite Trader Funding is the 1-Step Evaluation starting at $75. It uses either a trailing or static drawdown, allows news trading, and charges a $75 flat reset fee if you breach. That is standard prop firm territory and is the plan most traders will actually buy.

Fast Track is the version aimed at traders who want to compress the evaluation window. It is available at $100K and $250K account sizes, priced from $75 up to $175, with simplified rules and no minimum trading days. Elite Trader Funding markets this as capable of achieving funding in a single day, which is credible mechanically but only if you hit the profit target and do not breach.

Diamond Hands is a different animal. It is a $365 monthly subscription tied to a $100K account with a $5,000 profit target and a $3,500 end-of-day trailing drawdown. There is no reset fee because the model is ongoing access. If you cycle through evaluations frequently and pay reset fees each time, the math on Diamond Hands can work out cheaper. If you are a one-and-done trader, it is overkill.

Direct to Funded skips the evaluation entirely. You pay $599 for a $25K account or $699 for a $50K account and start immediately in sim-funded status. There is no target to hit before you are funded, which sounds attractive until you consider that the account sizes are smaller and the upfront cost is the highest on the menu. This is for traders who have already proven themselves at other firms and simply want to shortcut the qualifying process.

The menu is genuinely useful, but Elite Trader Funding does very little hand-holding to help you pick. Two of the six paths are not even fully described in the base information here, which is a fair reflection of how the buyer experience actually feels.

## What the $80 Monthly Activation Fee Actually Costs You

One cost that easy to miss until it hits: once you are funded, there is an $80 per month activation fee. This is on top of whatever you paid for the evaluation. Elite Trader Funding is not unique in charging an ongoing fee on funded accounts, but it is worth pricing out honestly. If you trade a sim-funded account for a year without hitting a payout large enough to cover it, that is $960 in ongoing overhead against your profits.

Combined with the reset fee structure on the standard evaluations, the true cost of trading with Elite Trader Funding is not the $75 sticker price. It is the $75 plus any resets plus $80 a month for as long as you hold the funded account. Traders who plan to run multiple accounts should multiply accordingly, and the September 2025 reduction of the maximum active sim-funded accounts from 20 down to 5 caps how far you can scale on that dimension.

## The 100% Profit Split on the First $12,500

Elite Trader Funding pays 100% of profits on the first $12,500 in lifetime sim-funded profits. That is a meaningful headline number and it is one of the more genuinely trader-friendly terms in the industry. After the first $12,500 the split reverts to whatever the firm's standard payout structure dictates, which is where the fine print stops being as favorable.

The practical read: the 100% on the first $12,500 is a real perk if you are early in your funded career and want to compound quickly. It is not a lifetime 100% split, and no serious prop firm offers one. Read it as a strong onboarding incentive rather than a long-term economic advantage.

## The LIVE ELITE Question Nobody Answers Clearly

Elite Trader Funding advertises a LIVE ELITE program that graduates sim-funded traders to a live-capital account. The criteria for that invitation are not clearly published. Sim-funded traders looking at Elite Trader Funding as a stepping stone to real capital should know upfront that the promotion path is opaque, at the firm's discretion, and cannot be planned around in the same way you can plan a scaling schedule at some competitors. If real capital is your explicit goal, this is a genuine weakness of the model and worth raising in support before you commit.

## Why the Futures-Only Restriction Kills This for Most Traders

Elite Trader Funding trades futures. Full stop. No spot forex, no equities, no options, no spot crypto. If you are a forex trader looking at prop firms, this is not your firm. If you trade equity options or want to scale a mixed strategy, the same. The category is prop firms broadly, but Elite Trader Funding sits specifically in the futures prop sub-segment alongside Topstep, Apex Trader Funding and Bulenox.

Inside futures, the platform integration is broker-driven and the rules accommodate news trading, which is more permissive than several competitors. Paper trading is available for practice, and the account offers basic performance analytics and risk management tools. There is no built-in journaling, no AI analysis, no backtesting engine, no education library and no API access. Elite Trader Funding is not trying to be a research platform. It is a rules engine and a payout mechanism sitting on top of a broker.

## Discounts, Reset Culture and How Traders Actually Buy This

Elite Trader Funding runs promotional discounts of 50 to 90 percent frequently enough that paying full sticker on an evaluation is unusual. That changes the real cost math meaningfully. A $150 evaluation at a 70% discount lands at $45, which reframes the reset economics: cheap evaluations mean traders are more willing to blow accounts and buy fresh ones, which is exactly how the firm makes money on the evaluation side.

If you are comparison shopping, wait for a promo before pulling the trigger on Elite Trader Funding. Paying full price is almost never necessary, and this is documented in the firm's own marketing cadence.

## Who Elite Trader Funding Fits and Who Should Pass

Elite Trader Funding fits futures day traders who want optionality in evaluation format, scalpers who need news trading permitted, and experienced traders who value the Direct to Funded shortcut or the Diamond Hands subscription model. It works for traders who plan to run one to three sim-funded accounts and take steady payouts against the 100% first-$12,500 tier.

It does not fit traders in any market other than futures, traders who want a clear published path to live capital, or traders who want a single simple evaluation without a menu of format choices. Topstep offers a more standardized experience, Apex offers similar futures-focused funding with different rules, and Earn2Trade or Bulenox may fit specific rule preferences better. Elite Trader Funding earns its place on the shortlist through breadth of options and aggressive discounting, not through any single dominant feature.

The honest read: this is a solid mid-tier futures prop firm with a genuinely useful menu of paths, a real 100% split incentive on early profits, and a real set of caveats around activation fees, the LIVE ELITE opacity and the account cap reduction. Price your total cost carefully, pick the evaluation format that matches your actual behavior, and wait for a promo.
affiliate_commission: Up to 15% on 1-Step evaluations (first 6 months), 7% on Fast Track, $5 per reset fee

company_background: Elite Trader Funding LLC is a futures-only prop firm founded in 2022 and headquartered in Wilmington, Delaware. The company has built its position in the futures prop segment through an unusually wide menu of six evaluation formats and frequent promotional pricing rather than a single flagship offering. It competes directly with Topstep, Apex Trader Funding and Bulenox in the sim-funded futures trader space.
